title: pterospartum tridentatum subsp. cantabricum
intro: Pterospartum tridentatum subsp. cantabricum, a species of flowering plant in the family Rosaceae, is also known as the Cantabrian broom or Spanish broom. It has been used in traditional medicine to treat various ailments due to its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and diuretic properties. The plant has been associated with several health benefits, such as reducing fever, relieving digestive issues, and supporting the urinary system. In herbal preparations, Pterospartum tridentatum subsp. cantabricum is often used to make teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are used to treat a range of health conditions.
